page: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The instance features 32 virtual cores with 32 physical cores and a single thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address. It does not feature any dedicated GPU hardware. The AMD EPYC-Turin processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptographic acceleration such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, though it lacks Intel-specific AMX features.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 3056.39 in stress-ng tests, which places it in the top 25% of tested instances. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng score of 48789.2, placing it in the middle 50%. Because the CPU allocation is shared, this instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require large memory and core counts but do not demand sustained, dedicated 100% CPU utilization.

This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, memory-intensive staging environments, web serving, and moderate database operations where shared CPU resource limits are acceptable.
